sections.heading: Overview
sections.paragraph: The pharmaceutical landscape achieved a historic milestone with the U.S. Food and Drug Administration approval of Veppanu (vepdegestrant), the first proteolysis-targeting chimera (PROTAC) therapy to reach the market. Developed by Arvinas and Pfizer, Veppanu is indicated for adults with metastatic, estrogen receptor-positive, HER2-negative breast cancer harboring ESR1 mutations who have progressed following endocrine therapy. Regulatory validation was grounded in Phase 3 VERITAC-2 trial data, which demonstrated a median progression-free survival of 5 months compared to 2.1 months with fulvestrant, alongside an objective response rate of 19 percent versus 4 percent. Concurrently, Arvinas reported positive Phase 1 data for ARV-102, an oral PROTAC targeting LRRK2 in Parkinson’s disease, achieving greater than 50 percent LRRK2 degradation in cerebrospinal fluid. While the approval establishes the clinical viability of targeted protein degradation, Arvinas and Pfizer are actively seeking a third-party licensing partner to manage commercialization economics. The approval also included Guardant360 CDx as a companion diagnostic to identify eligible patients. These developments signify the transition of PROTAC technology from experimental modalities to approved therapeutics, although commercial execution challenges remain as the companies navigate the post-approval partnership landscape to maximize the drug’s potential in a competitive breast cancer treatment environment.

points.text: Arvinas — Veppanu (vepdegestrant) — ESR1m, ER+/HER2- Advanced Breast Cancer — FDA Approval as first PROTAC
Arvinas — ARV-102 — Parkinson’s Disease — Phase 1 Data showing >50% LRRK2 degradation in CSF
Arvinas: Clinical-stage biotechnology pioneering PROTAC protein degrader platform for oncology and neurodegenerative diseases.
Pfizer: Longstanding partnership partner with Arvinas for co-development and commercialization of Veppanu.
Guardant Health: Developer of Guardant360 CDx companion diagnostic approved for ESR1 mutation detection.
Veppanu: PROTAC estrogen receptor degrader, Phase 3 VERITAC-2 trial, median PFS 5 months vs 2.1 months (fulvestrant), ORR 19% vs 4%, HR 0.57.
ARV-102: PROTAC LRRK2 degrader, Phase 1 trial, Parkinson’s disease patients, >50% LRRK2 reduction in CSF at day 14 maintained through day 28, reduced CD68 and GPNMB biomarkers.
Arvinas and Pfizer: Seeking third-party licensing partner for Veppanu to split economics following FDA approval.
